summary: Tighten the SeisBench classify contract and add a post-run metric gate plus a one-pass retune ladder so executors do not stop at a merely runnable but over-split catalog.

atomic_operations.rationale: Round 1's first full run failed on `TypeError: object of type 'ClassifyOutput' has no len()` because the skill still described `model.classify()` as list-like output instead of a container with `.picks`.
atomic_operations.expected_effect: Executors extract `classify_output.picks` on the first pass and avoid the predictable API-contract retry.
atomic_operations.risk: Some non-picking models may expose additional fields beyond `.picks`, so users still need to inspect model-specific extras when relevant.

atomic_operations.rationale: After the API fix, the run was accepted with 222 predicted events, but the evaluator showed 0.468 precision against 102 catalog events even though F1 only barely cleared 0.6; the skill lacked any requirement to run a quality gate after the first successful association.
atomic_operations.expected_effect: Executors treat the first successful `results.csv` as a candidate, run the evaluator or a bounded sanity check, and retune once instead of stopping at a metric-blind completion.
atomic_operations.risk: The extra acceptance pass adds one evaluation step and can trigger a retune on borderline-but-passing runs.

atomic_operations.rationale: The remaining failure mode was over-splitting from a recall-first picker configuration, but the picker skill offered no concrete first retune knob before method switching.
atomic_operations.expected_effect: Executors raise PhaseNet thresholds before touching association or swapping methods, which should improve precision with one bounded retune pass.
atomic_operations.risk: Higher picker thresholds can reduce recall if the first pass was already conservative.
